note: Stage 7 latent-only decoder: recover the scored content from the opaque quantised internal-state levels alone (directly-encoded content tokens withheld), so held-out and counterfactual slices become genuine faithfulness tests rather than the schema-aware round-trip the standard local reporter performs. See ROADMAP 'Sharper decoder caveat' / 'Planned next step' and NEXT_STEPS 'Current Focus'.
disposition: Implemented and runnable in CI (unit test confirms the decoder recovers content from internal-state levels and provably ignores the exact-content fields on synthetic data where content is recoverable). On the current discrete-attention checkpoint the latent-only decoder does NOT clear the faithful-access bar: a small, non-robust current-content advantage appears on the 8-example slice (+0.125 to +0.25, growing with interface width) but it vanishes on the larger 16-example slice and on the cue-switch / intervention slices, and memory/content-only content is never recovered above observation (content_supported = false everywhere). Honest reading: the coarse opaque latent interface on this checkpoint carries marginal current-attended-content signal at best and no reliable remembered/counterfactual content. This BOUNDS the Stage 7 faithful-access claim to the schema-aware round-trip reporter and leaves the genuine faithfulness leg open. Per the plan, this is a real negative-to-marginal result, not a hidden failure: the remaining routes are a richer latent interface on a checkpoint whose state more separably encodes remembered content, or the external API LLM / VLM path (quota/model-blocked).
